claims.claim: LFP cells have a higher failure temperature of approximately 250°C compared to NMC cells which fail at around 200°C under thermal abuse conditions.
claims.quote: It is apparent that LFP has a higher failure temperature of around 250°C compared to other chemistries. This indicates that LFP batteries have relatively better resilience to elevated heat stress. Failing temperature for LFP have has been reported to be around 200°C while that for the NMC is reported to be around 150°C

claims.claim: LFP cells smoke for more than 20 minutes under thermal abuse conditions, compared to approximately 5 minutes for NMC cells.
claims.quote: Compared to other cells, which smoked for around 5 min, the smoking duration for LFP exceeds 20 min

claims.claim: NMC1 and NMC3 produce the highest CO emissions, averaging over 150 L/kWh at 100% SOC, with peak emission rates surpassing 2 L/min, while LTO averages only 10–12 L/kWh.
claims.quote: NMC1 and NMC3 have the highest emission rate with more than 150 L/kWh at 100 % SOC, and peak emission rates surpassing 2 L/min... NMC1 averaging at 150 L/kWh while LTO averaging at 10–12 L/kWh

claims.claim: LFP cells emit up to 4 L/kWh of HF at 100% SOC, significantly more than NMC cells which range at 0.5–1.4 L/kWh.
claims.quote: LFP cells emit up to 4 L/kWh at 100 % SOC, which is significantly more than other chemistries. By comparison, the total emissions of NMC cells are lower, ranging at 0.5–1.4 L/kWh.

claims.claim: Battery chemistry influenced fire risk more than state of charge; NMC1 reaches a composite Risk Index of 4.2 at 75% SOC while LFP remains in the lower-risk band with RI approximately 2.3–3.3 across all SOC levels.
claims.quote: LFP stays consistently safe (RI ≈ 2.30–3.30) across the SOC window, reaffirming its superior thermal stability... NMC1 shows the steepest escalation, reaching RI = 4.20 at 75 % SOC and remaining high at full charge (RI = 3.99)
